Buyer's Guide

How to Choose Between a Congo and Timneh

By Mark Benjamin · 8 min · CongoAfricanGreys.com
CongoTimnehComparePick OneBirds

Congo African Grey: The Talker

The Congo (Psittacus erithacus) — pale grey body, jet-black beak, scarlet tail. They mature into confident, dramatic personalities and are widely considered the world's most accomplished talking parrot.

Key Takeaway
Congos talk more, but Timnehs bond faster. First-time owners often prefer Timnehs.

Timneh: Calmer, Earlier Talker

Smaller, darker — charcoal body, horn-colored upper beak, maroon tail. Timnehs start talking earlier (often before 6 months) and tend to be steadier, less prone to one-person bonding.
